name: Brazil
contextBlurb: Brazil's central bank issued a three-part crypto rule set in Nov 2025—Res. BCB 519, 520 and 521—that brings virtual-asset service providers (VASPs) under BCB authorisation and prudential oversight and classifies fiat-pegged crypto (stablecoin) transactions as foreign-exchange/capital-markets operations. The framework introduces licensing, governance/consumer-protection and AML/CFT requirements; minimum capital bands are roughly US$2–7 million depending on activity. Resolutions 519, 520 and 521 entered into full force on 2 February 2026; reporting of covered FX/VA operations to the BCB is required from 4 May 2026. Foreign firms serving Brazilian clients must obtain local authorisation.

legislation.title: Res. BCB 519, 520 & 521 entered into force, establishing a comprehensive VASP licensing and prudential regime and treating stablecoin transactions as FX operations.
BCB issues Res. BCB 519, 520 & 521 (crypto/VASP rulebook; FX treatment for fiat-pegged stablecoins).
Executive submits Bill 5,582/2025 (“anti-faction” bill) to Congress to allow pre-trial liquidation of seized crypto.
“Operation Deep Hunt” dismantles a R$164M crypto-laundering syndicate.

news.headline: Rio Times – Brazil's New Cryptocurrency Regulations in Force (February 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Brazil's central bank issued a three-part crypto rule set in Nov 2025—Res. BCB 519, 520 and 521—that brings virtual-asset service providers (VASPs) under BCB authorisation and prudential oversight and classifies fiat-pegged crypto (stablecoin) transactions as foreign-exchange/capital-markets operations. The framework introduces licensing, governance/consumer-protection and AML/CFT requirements; minimum capital bands are roughly US$2–7 million depending on activity. Resolutions 519, 520 and 521 entered into full force on 2 February 2026; reporting of covered FX/VA operations to the BCB is required from 4 May 2026. Foreign firms serving Brazilian clients must obtain local authorisation.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Brazil classifies operations with fiat-pegged crypto (stablecoins) within the foreign-exchange and international-capital perimeter under Resolução BCB nº 521/2025, issued alongside Res. 519/2025 and 520/2025.
